title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Type-System Trace: Transactional Inbox
summary: A consumer uses an in-memory set, marks messages complete before effects, reuses IDs across tenants, and assumes an idempotency key makes non-transactional email and payment calls exactly once. Planned artifact: Add and verify the transactional inbox increment in durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Transactional Inbox · Theory Transactional Inbox
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1034 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then close the channel while one acknowledgement decision is still pending. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
